plugins.name: build-chrome-extension
plugins.description: Building or debugging a Chrome extension with Manifest V3 — service workers, content scripts, popup/sidepanel, messaging, storage, permissions, testing, or Web Store publishing

plugins.name: build-convex-clerk-swiftui
plugins.description: Building a SwiftUI app with Convex backend and Clerk auth — covers reactive subscriptions, TypeScript server functions, schema design, auth integration, and iOS/macOS production patterns

plugins.name: build-copilot-sdk-app
plugins.description: Building TypeScript applications with the GitHub Copilot SDK (@github/copilot-sdk), including sessions, tools, streaming, hooks, custom agents, or BYOK

plugins.name: build-daisyui-mcp
plugins.description: Building UI with daisyUI components, converting Figma designs or screenshots to daisyUI code, or generating themes using the daisyui-blueprint MCP server

plugins.name: build-hcom-systems
plugins.description: Building applications or automation systems on hcom — multi-agent architectures, custom workflow scripts, CI/CD integration, or understanding hcom internals like hooks, SQLite, relay, and events

plugins.name: build-langchain-ts-app
plugins.description: Building TypeScript agents, RAG pipelines, or tool-calling workflows with LangChain.js and LangGraph

plugins.name: build-mcp-use-agent
plugins.description: Building TypeScript AI agents with mcp-use MCPAgent — configuration, LLM integration, streaming, structured output, observability, and production deployment

plugins.name: build-mcp-use-apps-widgets
plugins.description: Building or fixing an mcp-use server that renders React widgets and needs useWidget, useCallTool, streaming, CSP, or ChatGPT/MCP Apps compatibility

plugins.name: build-mcp-use-client
plugins.description: Building MCP client applications with the mcp-use TypeScript SDK — MCPClient, sessions, tools, resources, React hooks, code mode, and authentication

plugins.name: build-mcp-use-server
plugins.description: Building or extending TypeScript MCP servers with the mcp-use library — tools, Zod schemas, resources, prompts, transports, OAuth, sessions, testing, and deployment

plugins.name: build-openclaw-plugin
plugins.description: Building OpenClaw plugins — registering tools, channels, model providers, writing plugin manifests, and configuring tool profiles

plugins.name: build-openclaw-skill
plugins.description: Creating, testing, or publishing OpenClaw skills and need SKILL.md structure, metadata gating, reference routing, or ClawHub guidance

plugins.name: build-openclaw-workflow
plugins.description: Building OpenClaw automation — Lobster typed workflows, cron scheduled jobs, LLM task chains, and browser automation

plugins.name: build-raycast-script-command
plugins.description: Building or repairing Raycast Script Commands in Python or Bash and need correct metadata, output modes, arguments, discovery, or runtime behavior

plugins.name: build-skills
plugins.description: Creating or substantially revising a Claude skill and need workspace-first evidence, remote comparison, and repo-fit synthesis before writing SKILL.md

plugins.name: build-supastarter-app
plugins.description: Extending a Supastarter app with App Router pages, oRPC procedures, Better Auth flows, Prisma/Drizzle data work, billing, storage, or monorepo package changes

plugins.name: convert-snapshot-nextjs
plugins.description: Converting live websites or saved HTML snapshots into grounded Next.js pages with browser capture, self-hosted assets, extracted styles, and screenshot-led fidelity verification

plugins.name: convert-vue-nextjs
plugins.description: Converting Vue 2/3 or Nuxt components to Next.js App Router with React, including component mapping, state migration, routing, SSR, or ecosystem packages

plugins.name: debug-tauri-devtools
plugins.description: Debugging a Tauri app with CrabNebula DevTools — inspecting Rust logs, IPC calls, runtime config, plugin permissions, or bundled assets

plugins.name: develop-clean-architecture
plugins.description: Applying Clean Architecture layers, dependency direction, DDD entities, use cases, or strict TypeScript patterns to structure or audit a codebase

plugins.name: develop-macos-liquid-glass
plugins.description: Building, modernizing, or reviewing macOS SwiftUI code with Apple's Liquid Glass design system — glass toolbars, sidebars, windows, NSGlassEffectView, or migrating from NSVisualEffectView

plugins.name: develop-typebox-fastify
plugins.description: Building Fastify REST APIs with TypeBox schemas, type-provider-typebox, route validation, OpenAPI generation, plugins, hooks, inject() testing, or Drizzle/Prisma database integration

plugins.name: develop-typescript
plugins.description: Writing or reviewing framework-agnostic TypeScript and need strict typing, tsconfig/lint decisions, safer refactors, or guidance on generics, unions, and typed boundaries

plugins.name: enhance-prompt
plugins.description: Enhancing a prompt before sending it to an LLM or coding agent — adds narrative structure, thinking-steering, failure pre-emption, halt conditions, and verification layers

plugins.name: enhance-skill-by-derailment
plugins.description: Improving a skill's instructional quality by having a subagent use it on a real task, reading the execution trace to find breaks, and applying fixes

plugins.name: extract-saas-design
plugins.description: Forensically documenting the visual system of an existing SaaS dashboard or admin UI into structured design docs, not rebuilding or redesigning it

plugins.name: init-agent-config
plugins.description: Generating, auditing, or migrating repo-grounded AGENTS.md and CLAUDE.md files, especially for dual-file setups, multi-agent compatibility, or converting existing agent config

plugins.name: init-openclaw-agent
plugins.description: Configuring an OpenClaw agent workspace or runtime policy: workspace files, tool profiles, allow/deny lists, skills loading, memory, or security settings

plugins.name: init-review
plugins.description: Creating or refining AI code review config for GitHub Copilot, Devin Bug Catcher, Greptile, or any combination for a repository

plugins.name: optimize-mcp-server
plugins.description: Optimizing, auditing, or enhancing an MCP server's tool quality, error handling, security, context efficiency, or production readiness for any MCP SDK

plugins.name: optimize-swift-linter
plugins.description: Writing, reviewing, or setting up Swift code quality tooling for macOS/iOS apps — covers SwiftLint, SwiftFormat, Airbnb rules, architecture, testing, and platform-specific patterns

plugins.name: plan-issue-tree
plugins.description: Planning a project as GitHub Issues with sub-issues, wave-based execution order, labels, and dependency wiring via the gh CLI

plugins.name: publish-npm-package
plugins.description: Automating npm publishing via GitHub Actions and need auth, versioning, provenance, or workflow-template choices

plugins.name: review-pr
plugins.description: Reviewing a GitHub pull request or branch diff for merge readiness with high-signal findings grounded in stated goals and changed-file clusters

plugins.name: run-agent-browser
plugins.description: Driving the agent-browser CLI for browser navigation, DOM-grounded interaction, session/tab management, screenshots, data extraction, or repeatable web app workflows

plugins.name: run-comprehensive-research
plugins.description: Orchestrating multi-domain research across official docs, community forums, and practitioner experience with parallel researcher agents producing structured, implementation-ready documentation

plugins.name: run-github-repo-evaluate
plugins.description: Evaluating GitHub repositories for quality, maturity, and maintainer credibility using GraphQL batch queries, REST signals, and code-level analysis

plugins.name: run-github-repo-search
plugins.description: Searching GitHub for repositories matching a technology, pattern, library, or framework using diverse search hypotheses and gh CLI

plugins.name: run-github-scout
plugins.description: Finding the best GitHub repos for a technology need: searches, evaluates, and ranks repos using subagent swarms with automatic retry when results are weak

plugins.name: run-hcom-agents
plugins.description: Orchestrating multiple AI coding agents via hcom — launching headless agents, messaging between them, or writing multi-agent workflow scripts with patterns like worker-reviewer loops

plugins.name: run-issue-plan
plugins.description: Executing a project plan stored as GitHub Issues with sub-issues by reading the tree, dispatching subagents wave-by-wave, and tracking completion

plugins.name: run-openclaw-agents
plugins.description: Orchestrating OpenClaw multi-agent systems — spawning sub-agents, managing sessions, ACP routing, and cross-agent messaging

plugins.name: run-openclaw-deploy
plugins.description: Deploying OpenClaw to production — Docker containers, VPS setup, security hardening, gateway management, and monitoring

plugins.name: run-playwright
plugins.description: Controlling a live browser with @anthropic-ai/playwright-cli for navigation, forms, screenshots, tab workflows, or interactive debugging from the terminal

plugins.name: run-research
plugins.description: Researching a bug, library choice, architecture trade-off, or technical question where current web evidence, Reddit experience, and multi-source synthesis matter

plugins.name: test-by-mcpc-cli
plugins.description: Testing or debugging MCP servers with mcpc CLI across stdio, HTTP stateless, and HTTP stateful transports — tool calling, schema validation, and automated test scripts

plugins.name: use-skill-dl-util
plugins.description: Searching, discovering, and downloading AI coding skills using skill-dl with Serper-powered search and batch downloads
